source.documentTitle: 2026 ANNUAL TEACHING PLANS: ENGLISH MATHEMATICS: GRADE 4

units.topic: Revision of Grade 3 work (to be integrated into the topics accordingly) – WHOLE NUMBERS

subtopics.heading: Number range for counting, ordering, comparing and representing, as well as place value of digits
concepts: Count forwards and backwards in 2s, 3s, 5s, 10s, 25s, 50s, 100s between 0 and at least 10 000
Order, compare and represent numbers to at least four-digit numbers
Represent odd and even numbers to at least 1 000.
Recognise the place value of digits in whole numbers to at least four-digit numbers
Round off to the nearest 10, 100 and 1 000

prerequisites: Counting ordering, comparing and representing the place value of three-digit numbers up to 800
Recognise the place value of digits in whole numbers to at least three-digit numbers up to 800
Round off to the nearest 10

subtopics.heading: Write number sentences to describe problem situations
Solve and complete number sentences by:
concepts: ‒ Inspection
‒ Trial and improvement
‒ Checking the solution by substitution

subtopics.heading: Properties of whole numbers
concepts: Recognize and use the commutative, associative and distributive properties of operations with whole numbers
0 in terms of its additive property

prerequisites: Multiply 2, 3, 4, 5 and 10 to at least 100
Divide numbers to 100 by 2, 3, 4, 5 and 10
Use of appropriate symbols (+, −, ×, ÷, =)

subtopics.heading: Number range for calculations
concepts: Addition and subtraction of whole numbers of at least 4-digits

subtopics.heading: Calculation techniques
concepts: Use any two techniques to perform and check written and mental calculations of whole numbers including:
‒ Estimation
‒ Building up and breaking down numbers
‒ Using a number line
‒ Rounding off and compensating
‒ Using addition and subtraction as inverse operations

subtopics.heading: Solving problems
concepts: Solve problems involving whole numbers, including the following:
─ Financial contexts
─ Measurement contexts

prerequisites: Counting, ordering, comparing and representing the place value of three-digit numbers up to 800
Add up to 800
Subtract from 800
Recognise the place value of digits in whole numbers to at least 800
Round off to the nearest 10, 100 and 1 000
Adding and subtracting units, multiples of 10 and multiples of 100 to/from any three-digit number up to 800
notes: Note: Ensure that the strategies used do not compromise conceptual understanding
Note: Assignment to be completed in class within three hours
Whole numbers: Counting, ordering and representing numbers; Number sentences; Whole numbers: Addition and subtraction

subtopics.heading: Number range for calculations
concepts: Multiplication of one-digit numbers by numbers up to 10
Multiplication of at least a two-digit number by a one-digit number
Multiplication of at least a whole two-digit number by two-digit numbers

subtopics.heading: Calculation techniques
concepts: Use any two techniques to perform and check written and mental calculations of whole numbers including:
‒ Estimation
‒ Building up and breaking down numbers
‒ Doubling and halving
‒ Using multiplication and division as inverse operations

subtopics.heading: Number range for multiples and factors
concepts: Multiples of one-digit numbers to at least 100
Factors of one-digit whole numbers to at least 10
Factors of two-digit whole numbers to at least 100

subtopics.heading: Solving problems
concepts: Solve problems involving whole numbers, including:
‒ Financial contexts
‒ Measurement contexts
‒ Comparing two or more quantities of the same kind (ratio)
‒ Comparing two quantities of different kinds (rate)

prerequisites: Multiply 2, 3, 4, 5 and 10 to at least 100
Multiply 1 by one-digit whole numbers
Halving and doubling
Multiplication facts for units by multiples of 10 and 100
Building up and breaking down three-digit whole numbers.
Round off to the nearest 10 and estimate answers
notes: Note: Ensure that the strategies used do not compromise conceptual understanding

units.topic: FORMAL ASSESSMENT TASK – TEST: All topics

formalAssessment.label: Formal Assessment Task: Assignment
topicsCovered: Whole numbers: Counting, ordering and representing numbers
formalAssessment.conditions: Assignment to be completed in class within three hours

formalAssessment.label: Formal Assessment Task: TEST – All topics

notes: Note: Administer an investigation on any ONE of the Term 2 topics before teaching it

subtopics.heading: Number range for calculations
concepts: Division of at least a two-digit number by a one-digit number
Division of at least a whole three-digit number by one-digit numbers

subtopics.heading: Calculation techniques
concepts: Use any two of the range of techniques to perform and check written and mental calculations with whole numbers including:
‒ Estimation
‒ Building up and breaking down numbers
‒ Using multiplication and division as inverse operations

subtopics.heading: Solving problems
concepts: Solve problems in contexts involving whole numbers, including:
‒ Financial contexts
‒ Measurement contexts
‒ Comparing two or more quantities of the same kind (ratio)
‒ Comparing two quantities of different kinds (rate)
‒ Grouping and equal sharing with remainders

prerequisites: Divide numbers to 100 by 2, 3, 4, 5 and 10
Halving and doubling
Building up and breaking down three-digit whole numbers
Use multiplication and division as inverse operations
Round off to the nearest 10 and estimate answers
notes: Note: Ensure that the strategies used do not compromise conceptual understanding

units.topic: GEOMETRIC PATTERNS: Investigate and extend patterns

subtopics.heading: Investigate and extend geometric patterns
concepts: Investigate and extend geometric patterns looking for relationships or rules of patterns:
‒ in sequences not limited to a constant difference or ratio
‒ of the learners' own creation

subtopics.heading: Describe observed relationships or rules in learners' own words
Input and output values
concepts: Determine input values, output values and rules for the patterns and relationships using flow diagrams

subtopics.heading: Equivalent forms
concepts: Determine equivalence of different descriptions of the same relationship or rule presented in the following ways:
‒ Verbally
‒ In a flow diagram
‒ By a number sentence

prerequisites: Investigate and extend patterns
Describe patterns in own words

units.topic: NUMERIC PATTERNS: Investigate and extend patterns

subtopics.heading: Investigate and extend numeric patterns
concepts: Investigate and extend numeric patterns looking for relationships or rules of patterns:
‒ sequences involving a constant difference or ratio
‒ of learner's own creation

subtopics.heading: Describe observed relationships or rules for sequences involving constant difference or ratio in learners' own words
Input and output values
concepts: Determine input values, output values and rules for patterns and relationships:
‒ Flow diagrams
‒ Tables

subtopics.heading: Equivalent forms
concepts: Determine the equivalence of different descriptions of the same relationship or rule presented in the following ways:
‒ Verbally
‒ In a flow diagram
‒ In a table
‒ By a number sentence

prerequisites: Investigate and extend patterns
Describe patterns in own words

subtopics.heading: Range of shapes
concepts: Recognise, visualise and name 2-D shapes in the environment and geometric setting, focusing on regular and irregular polygons:
‒ Triangles
‒ Squares
‒ Rectangles
‒ Other quadrilaterals
‒ Pentagons
‒ Hexagons
‒ Heptagons
Recognise, visualise and name 2-D shapes in the environment and geometric setting, focusing on circles

subtopics.heading: Characteristics of shapes
concepts: Describe, sort and compare 2-D shapes in terms of the following:
‒ Straight and curved sides
‒ Number of sides

subtopics.heading: Further activities
concepts: Draw 2-D shapes on grid paper

subtopics.heading: SYMMETRY
concepts: Recognize, draw and describe line(s) of symmetry in 2-D shapes

prerequisites: Identify circles, triangles, squares and rectangles
Describe, sort and compare 2D shapes in terms of the following:
o Shape
o Straight sides
o Round sides

subtopics.heading: Range of objects
concepts: Recognize, visualize and name 3-D objects in the environment and geometric settings, focusing on:
‒ rectangular prisms,
‒ spheres
‒ cylinders
‒ pyramids

subtopics.heading: characteristics of objects
concepts: Describe, sort and compare 3-D objects in terms of
‒ shapes of faces
‒ flat and curved surfaces

subtopics.heading: Further activities
concepts: Make 3-D models using cut out polygons

prerequisites: Recognise and name 3-D objects in the classroom and in pictures
‒ ball shapes (spheres)
‒ box shapes (prisms)
‒ cylinders

units.topic: FORMAL ASSESSMENT TASK – TEST: All Term 1 & 2 topics

formalAssessment.label: Formal Assessment Task: Investigation
topicsCovered: Any ONE of the Term 2 topics (administered before teaching it)
formalAssessment.conditions: Administer an investigation on any ONE of the Term 2 topics before teaching it

formalAssessment.label: Formal Assessment Task: TEST – All Term 1 & 2 topics

notes: Note: The project must cover a combination of topics from Term 1 to 3 and must be completed before the end of Term 3

subtopics.heading: Describing and ordering fractions
concepts: Compare and order common fractions with different denominators (halves, thirds, quarters, fifths, sixths, sevenths and eighths)
Describe and compare common fractions in diagram form

subtopics.heading: Calculations with fractions
concepts: Recognise, describe and use the equivalence of division and fractions
Addition of common fractions with same denominators.

subtopics.heading: Solving problems
concepts: Solve problems in contexts involving fractions, including grouping and equal sharing

subtopics.heading: Equivalent forms
concepts: Recognise and use equivalent forms of common fractions (denominators which are multiples of one another)

prerequisites: Use and name unitary and non-unitary fractions in familiar contexts including halves, quarters, eighths, thirds, sixths and fifths
Recognise fractions in diagrammatic form
Recognise that two halves or three thirds make one whole and that one half and two quarters are equivalent
Write fractions as one half, two thirds

subtopics.heading: Collecting and organising data
concepts: Collect data using tally marks and tables for recording

subtopics.heading: Representing data
concepts: Draw a variety of graphs to display and interpret data including:
‒ pictographs (one-to-one correspondence) data and representation)
‒ bar graphs

subtopics.heading: Interpreting data
concepts: Critically read and interpret data represented in
‒ words
‒ pictographs
‒ bar graphs
‒ pie charts

subtopics.heading: Analysing data
concepts: Analyse data by answering questions related to data categories

subtopics.heading: Reporting data
concepts: Summarise data verbally and in short written paragraphs making predictions based on the data

children.heading: Probability experiments
concepts: Perform simple repeated events and list possible outcomes for experiments such as:
‒ tossing a coin
‒ rolling a die

prerequisites: Collect data about the class or school to answer questions posed by the teacher
Organise data supplied by teacher or workbook/textbook
Organise data in lists, tally marks and tables
Represent data in
‒ pictograph (limited to pictographs with one-to-one correspondence)
‒ bar graphs
Answer questions about data presented in
─ pictographs (limited to pictographs with one-to-one correspondence)
─ bar graphs

subtopics.heading: Practical measuring
concepts: Estimate and practically measure 3D objects using measuring instruments such as the following:
‒ bathroom scales
‒ kitchen scales and
‒ balances
Record, compare and order the mass in grams (g) and kilograms (kg);

subtopics.heading: Calculations and problem-solving
concepts: Solve problems in contexts involving mass
Convert between grams and kilograms limited to examples with whole numbers and fractions

prerequisites: Estimate, measure, compare, order and record mass
Compare, order and record the mass of commercially packaged objects

subtopics.heading: Build composite shapes
concepts: Put 2-D shapes together to make different composite 2-D shapes including some shapes with line symmetry.

subtopics.heading: Tessellations
concepts: Pack out 2-D shapes to make tessellated patterns including some patterns with line symmetry.

subtopics.heading: Describe patterns
concepts: Refer to lines, 2-D shapes, 3-D objects and lines of symmetry when describing patterns
‒ in nature
‒ from modern everyday life
‒ our cultural heritage

prerequisites: Recognise and draw line of symmetry in 2-D geometrical and non-geometrical shapes

units.topic: FORMAL ASSESSMENT TASKS – TEST: All Term 3 topics

formalAssessment.label: Formal Assessment Task: Project
topicsCovered: Combination of topics from Term 1 to 3
formalAssessment.conditions: The project must cover a combination of topics from Term 1 to 3 and must be completed before the end of Term 3

formalAssessment.label: Formal Assessment Task: TEST – All Term 3 topics

subtopics.heading: Reading time and time instruments
concepts: Read, tell and write time in 12-hour and 24-hour formats on both analogue and digital instruments in the following:
‒ Hours
‒ Minutes
‒ Seconds
Instruments include clocks watches and stopwatches

subtopics.heading: Calculations and problem solving with regard to time include the following:
concepts: Solve problems in contexts involving time
Calculation of the number of days between any two dates within the same or consecutive years
calculation of time intervals where time is given in:
‒ seconds and/or minutes
‒ minutes and/or hours
‒ hours and/or days
‒ days, weeks and/or months
‒ years and/or decades

prerequisites: Read dates on calendars
Place birthdays, religious festivals, public holidays, historical events and school events on a calendar
Use calendars to calculate and describe lengths of time in days or weeks or months, including the following:
o Converting between days and weeks
o Converting between weeks and months
Use clocks to calculate length of time in hours, half hours and quarters of an hour

subtopics.heading: Practical measuring
concepts: Estimate and practically measure 2D shapes and 3D objects using measuring instruments such as the following:
‒ Rulers
‒ Metre sticks
‒ Tape measures
‒ Trundle wheels
Record, compare and order lengths of shapes and objects in millimetres (mm) and centimetres (cm), metres (m) and kilometres (km)

subtopics.heading: Solving problems
concepts: Solve problems in contexts involving length
Convert between the following:
‒ Millimetres (mm) and centimetres (cm)
‒ Centimetres (cm) and metres (m)
‒ Metres (m) and kilometres (km)
Conversions limited to whole numbers and common fractions

prerequisites: Estimate, measure, compare, order and record length using non-standard measures, e.g. hand spans, paces, pencil lengths, counters, etc.
Describe the length of objects by counting and stating the length in informal units
Solve problems in contexts involving length
Convert between the following:
‒ Millimetres (mm) and centimetres (cm)
‒ Centimetres (cm) and metres (m)
‒ Metres (m) and kilometres (km)

subtopics.heading: Measure perimeter
concepts: Measure perimeter using rulers or measuring tapes

subtopics.heading: Measurement of area
concepts: Find areas of regular and irregular shapes by counting squares on grids in order to develop an understanding of square units

subtopics.heading: Measurement of volume
concepts: Find volume/ capacity of objects by packing or filling them in order to develop an understanding of cubic units

subtopics.heading: Practical measuring
concepts: Estimate and practically measure 3D objects using measuring instruments such as the following:
‒ Measuring spoons
‒ Measuring cups
‒ Measuring jugs
Record, compare and order the capacity and volume of 3D objects in millilitres (ml) and litres (ℓ)

subtopics.heading: Calculations and problem-solving
concepts: Solve problems in contexts involving capacity/volume
Convert between millilitres and litres (limited to examples with whole numbers and fractions)

prerequisites: Estimate, measure, compare, order and record the capacity of objects by measuring in litres, half litres and quarter litres

Describe the volume on jugs where the volume is near to a numbered millilitre gradation line using almost/ nearly/close to/a bit more than/more or less than/exactly the number of litres read on the jug

units.topic: FORMAL ASSESSMENT TASK – TEST: Term 1-4 topics

formalAssessment.label: Formal Assessment Task: TEST – Term 1-4 topics
